openFindings.title: Strict-validation success claims are stale
openFindings.description: The packet still claims strict validation passes, but the current validator run fails on spec documentation integrity because multiple docs reference a missing `.opencode/agent/speckit.md` surface.

openFindings.title: Canonical packet surfaces disagree on completion state
openFindings.description: The primary spec still reports `In Progress` and `_memory.continuity.completion_pct: 65`, while implementation-summary and graph metadata report the phase as closed and complete.

openFindings.title: description.json parentChain still points at the pre-renumber path
openFindings.description: The packet was renumbered to `001-search-and-routing-tuning`, but `parentChain` still records `010-search-and-routing-tuning`, so lineage metadata no longer mirrors the current folder path.

openFindings.title: Packet-local continuity identity still uses legacy 018/phase-004 markers
openFindings.description: Trigger phrases, fingerprints, and session identifiers still anchor the packet to `018 phase 004` even though the current packet lives under the 026/006/001 migration path. That identity drift weakens resume and retrieval fidelity for the moved packet.

openFindings.title: Three packet docs cite a non-existent `.opencode/agent/speckit.md` surface
openFindings.description: The packet preserves a reference to `.opencode/agent/speckit.md`, but that file does not exist in the checkout. These broken references are the immediate reason strict spec-doc integrity now fails.

openFindings.title: graph-metadata entity extraction is carrying low-signal prose fragments
openFindings.description: The derived entity list includes prose fragments such as `Backfilled Level`, `In Progress`, and `Problem Statement Several`, which lowers the signal quality of packet metadata used for search and review surfaces.
